text: Mlɩbla Bulu mlɩaa, “Fʋ atɔ bwɛhɛ ɩhɩɛ bʋ ifu, ɩbʋ wanwan! Fʋ tumi lɛhɩɛ moni. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋ alupʋ fɛ́ɛ́ bɔ́pʋ ifu da akpawunu fʋ ansɩtɔ.

ref: You are worshiped by everyone! We all sing praises to you.”
text: Ɔyɩtɔ aha fɛ́ɛ́ bósum fʋ. Bɔ́wa ɩlʋ kanfʋ fʋ, wa ɩlʋ kanfʋ fʋ ɩda.” Ɩya.

ref: Come and see the fearsome things our God has done!
text: Mlɩyɛkɩ tɔá Bulu labwɛ. Alabwɛ ofula ha anyankpʋsa.

ref: When God made the sea dry up, our people walked across, and because of him, we celebrated there.
text: Ɔlɛha ɔpʋ lɛdamli ɔsʋlʋ wʋlɩhɛ, anɩ anain bɛfa mʋ́ ayabitɔ. Ɩnʋ mʋ ɩwɩ lɔwa anɩ ansigyi.

ref: His mighty power rules forever, and nothing the nations do can be hidden from him. So don't turn against God.
text: Ɔdɛ iwie gyi brɛ fɛ́ɛ́ mʋ tumitɔ. Mʋ ansɩ dɩn ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́sʋ. Mʋsʋ akʋsʋlɩɩpʋ bʋmátsu ɩwɩ. Ɩya.

ref: All of you people, come praise our God! Let his praises be heard.
text: Ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩkanfʋ anɩ Bulu. Mlɩkanfʋ mʋ aha abʋnu.

ref: God protects us from death and keeps us steady.
text: Mʋ sʋ anɩtsie nkpa, ɔmɔkʋha anɩ ayabi ɩsrɛɩ.

ref: Our God, you tested us, just as silver is tested.
text: Oo Bulu, fʋlɔsɔ anɩ kɩ. Fɔsɔ anɩ kɩ fɛ alɩá bʋtɔpʋ ogya ŋlaɩn sika fututu, dɩnkɩ ipi lɛ mʋ́ ansɩtɔ.

ref: You trapped us in a net and gave us heavy burdens.
text: Fʋlɔpʋ mpʋsa lɛ anɩ, fɔpʋ atodwindwin sʋra anɩ.

ref: You sent war chariots to crush our skulls. We traveled through fire and through floods, but you brought us to a land of plenty.
text: Fɛha anɩ alupʋ bɛtsatsa anɩsʋ. Anɩlɔtsʋn ntsutso, tsʋn ogyatɔ, tamɛ séi fapʋ anɩ ba ɔtɩnɛá atɔ labusʋ.

ref: I will bring sacrifices into your house, my God, and I will do what I promised
text: Nɔ́pʋ atɔtɔhɛ-afɔdɩɛ ba fʋ otsiakpa. Nɛ́ka ɩkɔá notsula ha fʋ,

ref: when I was in trouble.
text: tɔá nobwii ɔnɔ blɩ; brɛá mbʋ ɩkplantɔ.

ref: I will sacrifice my best sheep and offer bulls and goats on your altar.
text: Nɔ́bɔ mbwɩá bawa nfɔ bɔ atɔtɔhɛ-afɔdɩɛ, pʋ nkpokpo ogyasi bɔ afɔdɩɛ ha fʋ. Pʋ nnantswie yinhɛ mʋ́a atrʋkpa bɔ afɔdɩɛ ha fʋ. Ɩya.

ref: All who worship God, come here and listen; I will tell you everything God has done for me.
text: Bulu ifu anyapʋ fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩbonu. Nɛ́blɩ tɔá alabwɛ ha mɩ.

ref: I prayed to the Lord, and I praised him.
text: Nosu kpoli mʋ. Mʋ kanfʋ ɩdɩn mɩ ɔnɔsʋ.

ref: If my thoughts had been sinful, he would have refused to hear me.
text: Nɩ nesi lalahɛ wa mɩ ɩklʋntɔ a, tɛkɩ mɩ Wie mónu mɩ isu,

ref: But God did listen and answered my prayer.
text: tamɛ lɛlɛ mʋ́ a, Bulu lanu mɩ isu, sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.

ref: Let's praise God! He listened when I prayed, and he is always kind.
text: Nɛ́kanfʋ mʋ, tsufɛ omekina mɩ isu nu, ntɛ kina mʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka lɛ suna mɩ.

ref: Our God, be kind and bless us! Be pleased and smile.
text: Bulu owun anɩ nwɛ, oyula anɩ. Ɔwa awɩtɔlɛ ha anɩ. Ɩya.

ref: Then everyone on earth will learn to follow you, and all nations will see your power to save us.
text: Mɛnɩ bɛ́bɩ fʋ ɔdwɛla ɔsʋlʋsʋ; pʋ́ fʋ aha nkpahɔ ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́tɔ.

ref: Make everyone praise you and shout your praises.
text: Oo Bulu, ha ɔmá-ɔmá abʋkanfʋ fʋ. Ha ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́ abʋkanfʋ fʋ!

ref: Let the nations celebrate with joyful songs, because you judge fairly and guide all nations.
text: Ha ansɩ igyi ɔmá-ɔmá, abʋwa ansigyi ɩlʋ, tsufɛ fʋtegyi ɔmá-ɔmá asʋn ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ. Fʋtɛkpa ɔmágyiɔmá ánɩ́ ɩbʋ ɔsʋlʋsʋ. Ɩya.

ref: Make everyone praise you, God, and shout your praises.
text: Oo Bulu, ha ɔmá-ɔmá abʋkanfʋ fʋ. Ha ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́ abʋkanfʋ fʋ!

ref: Our God has blessed the earth with a wonderful harvest!
text: Ɔsʋlʋsʋ-atɔ lawa alɛ. Bulu, anɩ Bulu layula anɩ.

ref: Pray for his blessings to continue and for everyone on earth to worship our God.
text: Bulu layula anɩ, ɩlaha ɔyɩtɔ aha fɛ́ɛ́ bóbu mʋ.

ref: Do something, God! Scatter your hateful enemies. Make them turn and run.
text: Bulu kʋsʋ, fʋ alupʋ abʋdasaɩn. Ha fʋ alupʋ abʋsrɩ dalɩ fʋ ansɩtɔ.

ref: Scatter them like smoke! When you come near, make them melt like wax in a fire.
text: Fɛ alɩá afu tɔtsʋan ogyasi natɩ a, alɩ fʋgya amʋ́ nɩ. Fɛ alɩá ogya tɛŋlaɩn okpin a, alɩ aha lalahɛ bʋhɔ fʋ ansɩtɔ nɩ.

ref: But let your people be happy and celebrate because of you.
text: Tamɛ ha aha yile abʋgyi ɔdwɛ. Bʋfuli kpayi ansigyisʋ fʋ ansɩtɔ, hɩɛ nya ansigyi.

ref: Our God, you are the one who rides on the clouds, and we praise you. Your name is the Lord, and we celebrate as we worship you.
text: Mlɩwa ɩlʋ ha Bulu. Mlɩwa ɩlʋ kanfʋ mʋ ɩda. Mlɩtswɩ ɩlʋ ha ɔha amʋʋ ɔna nwʋlʋtasʋ amʋ. Mʋ ɩda gyi Nana Bulu. Mlɩtswɩ ope mʋ ansɩtɔ.

ref: Our God, from your sacred home you take care of orphans and protect widows.
text: Bulu tsie mʋ onuto otsiakpa. Mʋgyi awumbi ɔsɩ, otegyi bʋa asʋrapʋ.

ref: You find families for those who are lonely. You set prisoners free and let them prosper, but all who rebel will live in a scorching desert.
text: Ɔtɛha ɔha ánɩ́ ɔma ɔhaa tenya abusuan. Ɔtɛlɛ obu adɩpʋ, ha bʋtegyi ɩwɩ. Tamɛ ɔtɛha mʋsʋ akʋsʋlɩɩpʋ bʋtetsia ɔsʋlʋ wʋlɩhɛsʋ.

ref: You set your people free, and you led them through the desert.
text: Oo Bulu, brɛá fʋgya fʋ aha nkpa, mlɩna ɔsʋlʋkpan amʋsʋ a, Ɩya.

ref: God of Israel, the earth trembled, and rain poured down. You alone are the God who rules from Mount Sinai.
text: fɛha ɔsʋlʋ lɛkpɩnkɩ, nyankpʋ lotswie tsu ɔsʋsʋ, Sinai Bulu ansɩtɔ, Israel Bulu ansɩtɔ.

ref: When your land was thirsty, you sent showers to refresh it.
text: Fɛha nyankpʋ lɛhɩɛ tswie, latsinki fʋ siadɩɛá idewu.

ref: Your people settled there, and you were generous to everyone in need.
text: Fʋ aha bɔpʋ ɩnʋ bwɛ otsiakpa. Oo Bulu, fʋ awɩtɔlɛ sʋ fɔbʋla ahianfɔ.

ref: You gave the command, Lord, and a chorus of women told what had happened:
text: Anɩ Wie lɛlɛ ɔnɔ, atsɩ tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ bɛblɩ asʋn wanklaan amʋ.

ref: “Kings and their armies retreated and ran, and everything they left is now being divided.
text: Bɛblɩ bɛɛ, “Ɩsa ɔnɔ alɩɩpʋ fɛ́ɛ́ bɛsrɩ!” Atsɩá bʋbʋ ɩnʋ beye atɔá besi bun.

ref: And for those who stayed back to guard the sheep, there are metal doves with silver-coated wings and shiny gold feathers.”
text: Aha ánɩ́ bʋda akufa ɩbantɔ kʋraa bʋgyi fɛ lɔpɔá sika fututu bun mʋ abambasʋ, sika pɛpɛ ɩdɛ ogya kpa mʋ ɩwɩ.

ref: God All-Powerful, you scattered the kings like snow falling on Mount Zalmon.
text: Brɛá Bulu Otumipʋ amʋ lɛda awie saɩn a, Salmonbʋsʋ lofuli fɛ dantapu.

ref: Why is it jealous of Zion, the mountain you chose as your home forever?
text: Basanbʋá fʋbʋ nsiebi tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ, ntogyi sʋ fʋdɛ Sionbʋá Bulu lalɛ, otsie mʋ́sʋ lu? Kɩ, ɩnʋ Nana Bulu onuto obétsia brɛ fɛ́ɛ́ nɩ!

ref: When you, Lord God, appeared to your people at Sinai, you came with thousands of mighty chariots.
text: Bulu akeke akinkin ɩdʋn mpɩm-du lafa. Mʋ́ anɩ Wie lɔpʋtsua Sinai ba mʋ otsiakpa nɩ.

ref: When you climbed the high mountain, you took prisoners with you and were given gifts. Your enemies didn't want you to live there, but they gave you gifts.
text: Brɛá ɔlɔdʋ ɩbʋ fʋahɛ amʋ a, ɔlɛkpa ndoun buo ɩwɩ. Aha bekie mʋ atɔ. Mʋsʋ akʋsʋlɩɩpʋ kʋraa bekie mʋ atɔ. Nana Bulu obétsia ɩnʋ.

ref: We praise you, Lord God! You treat us with kindness day after day, and you rescue us.
text: Mlɩkanfʋ anɩ Wie. Mʋtɔsʋra anɩ atɔ sʋrahɛ ekekegyiɛkɛ. Bulu gyi anɩ nkpa ɔhɔpʋ. Ɩya.

ref: You always protect us and save us from death.
text: Nkpa ɔhɔpʋ anɩ Bulu gyi. Anɩ Wie Nana Bulu tɛlɛ anɩ tsu lowutɔ.

ref: Our Lord and our God, your terrible enemies are ready for war, but you will crush their skulls.
text: Tamɛ obésisri mʋ alupʋ nwun. Amʋ́ na bʋdɛ ɩpɔngyila bwɛ nɩ.

ref: You promised to bring them from Bashan and from the deepest sea.
text: Anɩ Wie ɔbɛɛ, “Nɛ́lakpa mlɩ alupʋ tsu Basan ba. Nɛ́lɛ amʋ́ tsu ɔpʋ-ibuntɔ ba.

ref: Then we could wash our feet in their blood, and our dogs could chew on their bones.
text: Mɛnɩ mlɛ́tsatsa mlɩ alupʋ amʋ obugyatɔ, mlɩ akɩaɩ ɛ béyinta amʋ́ obugya.”

ref: We have seen crowds marching to your place of worship, our God and King.
text: Oo Bulu, bawun fʋa fʋ ɔdɔm amʋ ɔnatɩ. Alɩá mɩ Bulu pʋ́ mɩ Wie, fɔpʋ fʋna fɔyɔ fʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ.

ref: Singers come first, and then the musicians, surrounded by young women playing tambourines.
text: Ɩlʋ awapʋ bʋgyankpa, nkpɛ afʋlɩpʋ bʋgya ɔma, mbitebi bʋbʋ amʋ́ nsɩnɛ bʋdɛ akatsa kpɩnkɩ.

ref: They come shouting, “People of Israel, praise the Lord God!”
text: Bʋdɛ ɩlʋ wa bɛɛ, “Mlɩkanfʋ Bulu mʋ ɔmá amʋ fɛ́ɛ́ ofiakpa. Mlɩ Israel abi-aná, mlɩkanfʋ Nana Bulu!”

ref: The small tribe of Benjamin leads the way, followed by the leaders from Judah. Then come the leaders from Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Amʋ́tɔ ɔtrahɛ Benyamin abusuan igyankpa, Yuda abusuan ahandɛ pʋ́ amʋ́ aha tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ bʋbuo, Sebulon mʋa Naftali abusuan ahandɛ ɛ bʋgya ɔma.

ref: Our God, show your strength! Show us once again.
text: Oo Bulu, suna fʋ ɔwʋnlɩn. Suna fʋ ɔwʋnlɩn, fɛ alɩá fɔbwɛ ha anɩ brɛ amʋ.

ref: Then kings will bring gifts to your temple in Jerusalem.
text: Fʋ ɔtswɛkpa ánɩ́ ɩbʋ Yerusalem sʋ awie bʋtekie fʋ atɔ.

ref: Punish that animal that lives in the swamp! Punish that nation whose leaders and people are like wild bulls. Make them come crawling with gifts of silver. Scatter those nations that enjoy making war.
text: Wa ɩyɩn wa Egyipte, ogyi fɛ ntsu-ɔnɔ ɔbwɩ mʋ́a ɔmá-ɔmá ánɩ́ bʋgyi fɛ nnantswie yinhɛá bʋbʋ amʋ́ abitɔ anfɩ. Tsatsa aha anfɩ bʋtekle dutɛ hɔ anfɩsʋ, afʋda ɔmá anfɩ bʋtekle ɩsa kɔ anfɩ saɩn.

ref: Force the Egyptians to bring gifts of bronze; make the Ethiopians hurry to offer presents.
text: Ha abʋpʋ kɔɔbrɩ tsua Egyipte ba. Etiopiafɔ abʋba ɩwɩasɩ ha fʋ Bulu.

ref: Now sing praises to God! Every kingdom on earth, sing to the Lord!
text: Mlɩ ɔsʋlʋsʋ iwie ogyikpa, mlɩwa ɩlʋ ha Bulu. Mlɩwa ɩlʋ kanfʋ anɩ Wie, Ɩya.

ref: Praise the one who rides across the ancient skies; listen as he speaks with a mighty voice.
text: bɩmbɩ ɔpá ɔsʋsʋsʋ ɔdɩnkapʋ amʋ. Kɩ, mʋ ɔmɛ lalɩn. Mʋ ɔmɛ kpɔnkpɔntɩ amʋ lalɩn.

ref: Tell about God's power! He is honored in Israel, and he rules the skies.
text: Mlɩbla aha alɩá Bulu tumi gyi. Mʋ numnyam ibun Israelsʋ, mʋ ɔwʋnlɩn ɩlɛlɩn ɔwan nwʋlʋtatɔ.

ref: The God of Israel is fearsome in his temple, and he makes us strong. Let's praise our God!
text: Bulu ɩwɩ bʋ ifu mʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ. Israel Bulu tɔpʋ mʋ tumi mʋ́a ɔwʋnlɩn ha amʋ́. Mlɩkanfʋ Bulu!

ref: Save me, God! I am about to drown.
text: Oo Bulu, hɔ mɩ. Ntsu labʋla wie mɩ ɔmɛtɔ.

ref: I am worn out from crying, and my throat is dry. I have waited for you till my eyes are blurred.
text: Nasu kpoli alɩɩ, ɩlapian mɩ. Mɩ ɔmɛ lapin. Nakɩ mɩ Bulu ɔkpa alɩɩ, mɩ ansibi lawu mɩ.

ref: There are more people who hate me for no reason than there are hairs on my head. Many terrible enemies want to destroy me, God. Am I supposed to give back something I didn't steal?
text: Aha ánɩ́ bʋdɛ mɩ lu kʋkʋ kpalɩ bʋtsɔ dʋn mɩ nwunsʋ-imi. Alupʋá bʋdɛ ofunu wa dɩnka, abʋmɔ mɩ bʋhɩɛ bʋdʋn mɩ. Bʋdɛ mɩ ka hɩɛ bɛɛ, mpʋ tɔá mmɔkʋtsu ba.

ref: You know my foolish sins. Not one is hidden from you.
text: Oo Bulu, fʋyin mɩ mimlatɔ. Mɩ lakpan meŋain fʋ.

ref: Lord God All-Powerful, ruler of Israel, don't let me embarrass anyone who trusts and worships you.
text: Oo anɩ Wie, Ɩsa Ɔnɔ Ɔlɩɩpʋ, Nana Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha pɛlɩ ɩkɩta fʋ ɔkpa akɩpʋ. Oo Israel Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha fʋ adunkapʋ abʋsʋpa.

ref: It is for your sake alone that I am insulted and blush with shame.
text: Tsufɛ fʋ sʋ besia mɩ, pɛlɩ lɛkɩta mɩ.

ref: I am like a stranger to my relatives and like a foreigner to my own family.
text: Namli ɔfɔɔ mɩ apiotɔ. Nabwɛ fɛ ɔha ánɩ́ bʋmeyin mɩ mɩ yin abitɔ.

ref: My love for your house burns in me like a fire, and when others insult you, they insulted me as well.
text: Fʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ asʋn ɩhɩɛ bʋ mɩ ogya. Fʋ asiabi fɛ́ɛ́ lɛbɛdɩnka mɩsʋ.

ref: I cried and went without food, but they still insulted me.
text: Brɛá nɛklɩ ɔnɔ su kpoli fʋ Nana Bulu a, besia mɩ.

ref: They sneered at me for wearing sackcloth to show my sorrow.
text: Brɛá nɔpʋ kpekpe bwɛ mɩ tati dɩdahɛ a, nemli blɩbi dɩnka amʋ́ ɔnɔsʋ.

ref: Rulers and judges gossip about me, and drunkards make up songs to mock me.
text: Mɩ ɩwɩ asʋn sɔɔn betsia blɩ dɩnsʋ. Nta abʋpʋ bɔwa mɩ ɩlʋtɔ.

ref: But I pray to you, Lord. So when the time is right, answer me and help me with your wonderful love.
text: Tamɛ mɩ mʋ́ fʋ Nana Bulu netsia su kpoli. Oo Bulu, brɛá iletsia fʋ a, pʋ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka pʋ́ fʋ nkpahɔá igyi ɔnɔkwalɩ sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.

ref: Don't let me sink in the mud, but save me from my enemies and from the deep water.
text: Lɛ mɩ lɛ ɔfrɔdɔ anfɩtɔ, mási mɩ ammɛ. Lɛ mɩ lɛ mɩ alupʋ anfɩ ɩbɩ. Bɩtɩa mɩ lɛ ntsu-ibun anfɩtɔ.

ref: Don't let me be swept away by a flood or drowned in the ocean or swallowed by death.
text: Máha ntsu anfɩ ɩbʋla kɩta mɩ, ankpada wie ntsu-ibuntɔ, ntɛ lowu ɩpʋ mɩ.

ref: Answer me, Lord! You are kind and good. Pay attention to me! You are truly merciful.
text: Nana Bulu, sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ, tsufɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka bʋ alɛ. Fʋ nwewun kpɔnkpɔntɩ amʋ sʋ damli kɩ mɩ.

ref: Don't turn away from me. I am your servant, and I am in trouble. Please hurry and help!
text: Mápʋ ansɩtɔ ŋain fʋ osumbi. Ɩladʋn mɩ, wa ɔsa sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.

ref: Come and save me from my enemies.
text: Kpʋkpɛ ba bɛka lɛ mɩ. Ka mɩ lɛ mɩ alupʋ ɩbɩtɔ.

ref: You know how I am insulted, mocked, and disgraced; you know every one of my enemies.
text: Fʋyin mɩ asiabi, fʋyin mɩ pɛlɩ pʋ́ mɩ nsʋpa. Fawun mɩ adʋn fɛ́ɛ́.

ref: I am crushed by insults, and I feel sick. I had hoped for mercy and pity, but there was none.
text: Amʋ́ asiabi labwiebwie mɩ, ɩlatu mɩ awútɔ. Nkɩ mbɛɛ, ɔkʋ obówun mɩ nwɛ, ntɛ kɩta ɩklʋn wa mɩ ɩwɩtɔ, tamɛ mmenya ɔhaa.

ref: Enemies poisoned my food, and when I was thirsty, they gave me vinegar.
text: Brɛa akʋn dɛ mɩ a, bɔwa atɔ atɔtɔ ha mɩ bɛɛ, ngyi. Brɛá ɔmɛwʋlɩ dɛ mɩ a, bɔpʋ nta-ɔdaɩ ha mɩ bɛɛ nnun.

ref: Make their table a trap for them and their friends.
text: Amʋ́ ɔpʋnʋ tswɩhɛ ɩbwɛ yibi, ɩkɩta amʋ́. Amʋ́ ɔdwɛ ogyikpa ɩbwɛ agbla, ɩkpa kɩta amʋ́.
